page.title: Marbella Sunset Snorkeling Combo: Swim, Snorkel & Watch the Sunset

data.meta_description: Book a Marbella sunset snorkeling combo from Puerto Banús. Swim, snorkel & watch the sunset from a private boat. From €749 for 2 hours. Reserve now.
data.summary: Combine afternoon snorkeling in Marbella's clear coves with a sunset cruise from Puerto Banús. Our private boats include skipper, drinks, snacks, and snorkel gear from €749 for 2 hours.
key_facts: Includes snorkel gear, skipper, fuel, insurance, IVA
Complimentary drinks: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava
Snorkel spots: Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, or Cabopino reef
Sunset views of La Concha mountain and Golden Mile
Max 11 passengers on standard boats; larger groups on request
Free cancellation up to 48 hours before departure

faq.q: What time does the sunset snorkeling combo start?
faq.a: We recommend a 16:00 or 17:00 departure depending on the season. This gives you 1–1.5 hours of snorkeling in daylight followed by a sunset cruise back to Puerto Banús. Sunset times vary from 18:00 (December) to 21:30 (June). We confirm the exact time when you book.
Yes, we provide masks, snorkels, and fins for all passengers. Just let us know your sizes when booking. Life jackets are also available for non-swimmers.

faq.q: Which boats are available for the sunset snorkeling combo?
faq.a: Our Tier A boats — the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) and Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) — are ideal for this trip. Both depart from Puerto Banús and include a skipper, drinks, snacks, and snorkel gear. Larger groups can charter a Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) from €4,719 for 4 hours.

We choose the best spot based on sea conditions and your preferences. Popular options include Cala del Faro near the Marbella lighthouse, Cala Cortés with its rocky reefs, or the Cabopino reef system. All are within 15–20 minutes from Puerto Banús.

faq.q: What is included in the price?
faq.a: The price covers a licensed skipper, fuel, snorkel gear, insurance, Spanish IVA (21%), and an open bar of water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava. Light snacks are also provided. There are no hidden fees.

faq.q: Can we bring our own food or drinks?
faq.a: Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and additional drinks. The boat has a small fridge and cooler. We recommend light snacks like fruit or sandwiches to enjoy during the sunset.

faq.q: Is the sunset snorkeling combo suitable for children?
faq.a: Absolutely. Children are welcome, and life jackets in all sizes are available. The snorkeling spots are sheltered and shallow (3–8m depth). Soft drinks and snacks are included for the whole family.

faq.q: What happens if the weather is bad?
faq.a: We monitor forecasts closely. If conditions are unsafe for snorkeling or navigation, we will offer you a full refund or reschedule to another date. Your safety is our priority.

data.body_html:
Combine the best of Marbella's coastline in one unforgettable afternoon: snorkel in crystal-clear coves, then watch the sun sink behind La Concha from the deck of your private boat. Our Marbella sunset snorkeling combo departs from Puerto Banús and includes everything you need — skipper, snorkel gear, drinks, snacks — from €749 for a 2-hour charter. Perfect for couples, families, or small groups who want a taste of the Mediterranean without a full-day commitment.

Why Choose the Sunset Snorkeling Combo?

Most boat trips in Marbella ask you to choose between a daytime swim stop or an evening sunset cruise. This combo gives you both in one efficient, relaxing outing. You get 60–90 minutes of snorkeling at a sheltered cove like Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés, followed by a gentle cruise back to Puerto Banús as the sky turns orange and pink. The boat handles the logistics — you just jump in the water and enjoy the view.

Your Boat Options

We run the sunset snorkeling combo on our Tier A boats:

  • Astondoa 40 (12.5m, up to 9 passengers) — €749 for 2 hours
  • Azimut 39 (12.5m, up to 11 passengers) — €749 for 2 hours

Both depart from Puerto Banús and include a licensed skipper, snorkel gear, fuel, insurance, and an open bar of water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava with light snacks. For larger groups (up to 12), consider our Mangusta 80 from €4,719 for 4 hours, which also includes a jet ski. See our full fleet for more options.

Snorkeling Spots We Visit

We select the best cove based on wind, swell, and visibility. Typical destinations include:

  • Cala del Faro — Rocky reef with bream and wrasse, 3–8m depth, 10 minutes from Puerto Banús
  • Cala Cortés — Sandy bottom with seagrass and occasional octopus, 4–6m depth
  • Cabopino reef — Artificial reef with diverse marine life, 15 minutes south

All spots are within 20 minutes of the marina, giving you maximum time in the water.

What's Included

ItemDetails
SkipperLicensed, English & Spanish speaking
Snorkel gearMasks, snorkels, fins (all sizes)
DrinksWater, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava
SnacksLight snacks (chips, olives, nuts)
FuelFull tank, no extra charge
InsurancePublic liability & passenger insurance
IVASpanish 21% VAT included

Sample Itinerary

Here's how a typical sunset snorkeling combo unfolds (departure at 16:00 in summer):

  • 16:00 — Meet at Puerto Banús, board your boat, safety briefing
  • 16:15 — Depart towards Cala del Faro (15 min cruise)
  • 16:30–17:45 — Snorkeling at the cove, with drinks on board
  • 17:45 — Head back towards Marbella coastline
  • 18:15–19:00 — Sunset cruise along the Golden Mile, with cava and music
  • 19:00 — Return to Puerto Banús

We can adjust timings to match sunset in your booking month.

Sunset Views from the Water

Marbella's coastline faces west, making it one of the best places on the Costa del Sol for sunset. From the boat you'll see the silhouette of La Concha mountain, the lights of the Golden Mile resorts, and the yachts at anchor. The water often turns a deep gold — a perfect photo moment. Pair it with a glass of cava and you have a Marbella evening to remember.

How to Book

Ready to reserve your Marbella sunset snorkeling combo? Contact us via WhatsApp or our booking form. We need your preferred date, group size, and any dietary requirements. Payment is via bank transfer or card. Book at least 48 hours in advance for best availability. For other experiences, check our experiences page or return to the homepage.

Frequently asked questions

What time does the sunset snorkeling combo start?

We recommend a 16:00 or 17:00 departure depending on the season. This gives you 1–1.5 hours of snorkeling in daylight followed by a sunset cruise back to Puerto Banús. Sunset times vary from 18:00 (December) to 21:30 (June). We confirm the exact time when you book.

Do you provide snorkeling equipment?

Yes, we provide masks, snorkels, and fins for all passengers. Just let us know your sizes when booking. Life jackets are also available for non-swimmers.

Which boats are available for the sunset snorkeling combo?

Our Tier A boats — the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) and Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) — are ideal for this trip. Both depart from Puerto Banús and include a skipper, drinks, snacks, and snorkel gear. Larger groups can charter a Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) from €4,719 for 4 hours.

Where do we go snorkeling?

We choose the best spot based on sea conditions and your preferences. Popular options include Cala del Faro near the Marbella lighthouse, Cala Cortés with its rocky reefs, or the Cabopino reef system. All are within 15–20 minutes from Puerto Banús.

What is included in the price?

The price covers a licensed skipper, fuel, snorkel gear, insurance, Spanish IVA (21%), and an open bar of water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava. Light snacks are also provided. There are no hidden fees.

Can we bring our own food or drinks?

Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and additional drinks. The boat has a small fridge and cooler. We recommend light snacks like fruit or sandwiches to enjoy during the sunset.

Is the sunset snorkeling combo suitable for children?

Absolutely. Children are welcome, and life jackets in all sizes are available. The snorkeling spots are sheltered and shallow (3–8m depth). Soft drinks and snacks are included for the whole family.

What happens if the weather is bad?

We monitor forecasts closely. If conditions are unsafe for snorkeling or navigation, we will offer you a full refund or reschedule to another date. Your safety is our priority.
